cataloging_subject.label: How the Wise Man Taught His Son: texts alpha, beta, and gamma
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the U-M/General Corpus electronic selection from Rudolf Fischer's 1889 edition: two edition title pages and Fischer's complete three edited version-groups on printed pp. 27-49. It is one anonymous Middle English advice poem represented through three reconstructed textual groupings, not three independent works, not one homogeneous critical text, and not a direct transcription of any single manuscript. Fischer's introduction, notes, and index are outside the electronic unit.

catalog_summary.title: How the Wise Man Taught His Son
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous
catalog_summary.date: Fifteenth century; first known witness conventionally dated about 1430; source edition 1889
catalog_summary.region: England; precise place of composition unknown

assertion.notes: A normalized conventional title. Manuscript wording and spelling vary, and the Cambridge witness uniquely uses 'goode man' in its heading.

agents.name: Rudolf Fischer
notes: Editor of the 1889 source edition and constructor of its alpha, beta, and gamma version texts.

scope.description: Underlying Middle English advice poem represented by all three version-groups

Underlying work, not any individual witness or edition

assertion.notes: The witnesses establish fifteenth-century circulation but do not securely date composition.

date_statements.display: Circa 1430 in Lambeth Palace Library MS 853

scope.description: Earliest conventionally dated witness among the six currently identified

assertion.notes: The date is a conventional manuscript date, not a composition date.
This broad English origin does not transfer the localized dialect or production place of any one witness to the abstract work.

scope.description: All three edited medieval version texts and their manuscript variants
Fischer's headings and apparatus retained in the electronic selection

form_statements.description: The work is an eight-line-stanza advice poem. Fischer's three texts have 24, 19, and 20 stanzas respectively in the XML hierarchy.

subject_statements.label: A father's moral and practical instruction to his son
Marriage, household governance, and treatment of dependants
Speech, truth, reputation, and civic responsibility

summaries.value: An anonymous Middle English father-to-son advice poem in which a wise man teaches a fifteen-year-old heir how to govern speech, work, worship, money, office-holding, marriage, servants, food and drink, tavern-going, gambling, charity, and preparation for death. Its practical household counsel is framed by Christian piety and the instability of worldly wealth.

CME00042 presents Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma texts with exhaustive paired-witness footnotes. Alpha is based on Cambridge Ff.2.38 with Harley 5396 variants; beta on Lambeth 853 with Balliol 354 variants; gamma chiefly on Harley 2399, with Ashmole 61 readings and three continuous stanzas supplied from the Ashmole branch.

The six witnesses differ in date, place, wording, completeness, and stanza inventory. The production place or dialect of one codex must not be assigned to the work as a whole. Fischer's Greek-letter texts are editorial groupings and gamma is explicitly composite rather than a surviving manuscript text.

extent.description: The XML body has three DIV2 version containers. Their base-text hierarchy contains 24, 19, and 20 LG stanzas and 192, 152, and 160 L elements, totaling 63 stanzas and 504 verse lines. The file also contains 436 NOTE elements, 100 line-number milestones, 25 PB elements, and two title-page DIV1 elements. These are representation metrics for Fischer's three editorial texts, not a single canonical work-line count or a claim that every witness has 504 lines.

content_parts.label: Text alpha
content_parts.summary: Fischer's 24-stanza, 192-line alpha version, based on Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 and collated with British Library Harley MS 5396.

locators.value: fols. 53r-54r; Fischer's legacy locator is fols. 45r-46r

content_parts.label: Text beta
content_parts.summary: Fischer's 19-stanza, 152-line beta version, based on Lambeth Palace Library MS 853 and collated with Balliol College MS 354.

content_parts.label: Text gamma
content_parts.summary: Fischer's 20-stanza, 160-line composite gamma version, chiefly based on British Library Harley MS 2399 and supplemented from the Ashmole 61 branch, including Ashmole-only stanzas placed as gamma 8, 12, and 13.
notes: Gamma is an editorial reconstruction and must not be described as a diplomatic transcription of Harley 2399 or Ashmole 61.

related_works.title: How the Good Wife Taught Her Daughter
notes: A related female-directed conduct poem often studied alongside this work; the two should not be assumed to have originated as one work or to be paired in every manuscript.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for CME00042.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for identifiers, title pages, Fischer's three version texts, printed pagination, base lines, variants, and German editorial notes.

evidence.citation: Namespace-aware structural inspection of CME00042.xml and comparison with the cached Fischer scan.
evidence.locator: FRONT, BODY/DIV1, three DIV2 version containers, LG, L, NOTE, MILESTONE, and PB
evidence.summary: Established three version parts, 63 LG stanzas, 504 base-text L elements, 436 notes, 100 line-number milestones, 25 page breaks, and source coverage of the two title pages plus printed pp. 27-49.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00042.
evidence.summary: Official item page identifying Fischer's 1889 edition and the alpha, beta, and gamma electronic contents.

evidence.citation: Rudolf Fischer, ed., How the Wyse Man Taught Hys Sone: In drei Texten (Erlangen and Leipzig: A. Deichert, 1889), complete scan.
evidence.locator: introduction pp. 1-26; edited texts pp. 27-49; notes pp. 50-64
evidence.summary: Primary source for the six-witness census, sigla, version grouping, working transcripts, base-witness choices, supplied Ashmole stanzas, editorial normalization, metre, and the exact source passages encoded by CME00042.

evidence.citation: Eve Salisbury, ed., The Trials and Joys of Marriage (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 2002), pp. 233-245.
evidence.summary: Modern scholarly edition and notes identifying the six witnesses, current locators, anonymous status, conduct-literature context, title variation, and selected textual differences.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series metadata page, How the Goode Man Taght Hys Sone.
evidence.summary: Current METS text metadata classifying the item as anonymous Middle English poetry and wisdom literature.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Ashmole 61, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, record manuscript_359.
evidence.summary: Exact institutional record for the Ashmole witness: title, incipit, explicit, omitted stanzas, DIMEV 3241, 1490-1510 date, possible Leicester origin, Rate's hand, paper support, and complete/partial digital surrogates.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Ashmole 61.
evidence.summary: Complete public codex surrogate and direct visual evidence for the Ashmole work portion, its continuous manuscript layout, Rate colophon, and fish drawing.

evidence.citation: Six-witness census reconciled from Fischer 1889, Salisbury 2002, and current institutional catalogs.
evidence.summary: Supports the six surviving witnesses and separates their dates, locators, and manuscript-specific production places from work-level metadata.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Hymns to the Virgin and Christ and Other Religious Poems, chiefly from Lambeth MS 853 (London: Early English Text Society, 1867).
evidence.summary: Historical edition describing Lambeth MS 853 as circa 1430 and printing the manuscript's contents; the date remains an attributed scholarly estimate.

evidence.citation: Letizia Vezzosi, 'L'educazione della fanciulla e la formazione del fanciullo: How the Good Wife taught Her daughter e How the Wise Man taught His son,' Filologia Germanica (2025), 183-210.
evidence.summary: Recent interpretation of the paired conduct traditions, household education, gendered advice, and Rate's reworking of the Ashmole version.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source-lineage manifest for CME00042.
evidence.summary: Provides the repository-to-U-M-to-Fischer chain, five working-transcript intermediaries, six witness mappings, facsimile access, and rights evidence used to qualify descriptive metadata.

open_questions.question: Can the poem's composition date and place be narrowed without borrowing the date or dialect of one witness?
next_steps: Review current linguistic and textual scholarship across all six witnesses rather than relying on Fischer's short dialect discussion.
Keep witness production dates and places separate from the abstract work.

open_questions.question: How should the alpha, beta, and gamma version-groups be related stemmatically under current scholarship?
next_steps: Reassess all six witnesses and Fischer's variants with current textual methods.
Do not treat Fischer's Greek-letter groupings as a proven single archetype or his composite gamma as an extant witness.

open_questions.question: Should the preferred title normalize 'wise' or preserve the Cambridge witness's unique 'good' wording?
next_steps: Retain the established normalized preferred title while preserving witness-specific titles and incipits.
Review DIMEV/NIMEV authority practice before changing the display title.

notes: Metadata describes the anonymous work and the CME edited selection separately from individual manuscripts, Fischer's edition, and modern supporting editions.
The complete Ashmole and Balliol facsimiles are recorded in the linked lineage manifest; this metadata file does not infer work-level geography or formatting from those two witnesses.
The DIMEV/NIMEV identifiers apply to the textual work/tradition; individual witness incipits and local titles remain separately recorded.
